Continue work on branch `fix/vr-pass-with-no-tests` in the existing worktree at `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/fix-vr-no-tests`. Do NOT create a new worktree.

Read the full plan at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-02-vr-docker-baselines.md` before doing anything else — it has the complete context.

The short version: three bugs need fixing before VR baselines can be generated:

1. **`playwright.config.ts`** — add `{projectName}` to `snapshotPathTemplate`: `'{testDir}/__snapshots__/{testFilePath}/{arg}-{projectName}{ext}'`
2. **`package.json`** — add `--ignore-snapshots` to `test:e2e` so visual tests don't fail the E2E run
3. **`scripts/vr-docker.mjs`** — pre-build the app on the host before launching Docker (the webServer build fails inside the container with ECONNREFUSED); run `APP_BASE_URL=/ yarn build && cp dist/client/_shell.html dist/client/index.html` on the host first, then pass the already-built dist into Docker so the container only needs to serve and test, not build

After fixing: run `yarn test:vr:update` to generate baselines, verify with `yarn test:vr`, commit everything, and push. Then open a PR targeting `master`.

# Executing Plans

## Overview

Load plan, review critically, execute all tasks, report when complete.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the executing-plans skill to implement this plan."

**Note:** Tell your human partner that Superpowers works much better with access to subagents. The quality of its work will be significantly higher if run on a platform with subagent support (such as Claude Code or Codex). If subagents are available, use superpowers:subagent-driven-development instead of this skill.

## The Process

### Step 1: Load and Review Plan
1. Read plan file
2. Review critically - identify any questions or concerns about the plan
3. If concerns: Raise them with your human partner before starting
4. If no concerns: Create TodoWrite and proceed

### Step 2: Execute Tasks

For each task:
1. Mark as in_progress
2. Follow each step exactly (plan has bite-sized steps)
3. Run verifications as specified
4. Mark as completed

### Step 3: Complete Development

After all tasks complete and verified:
- Announce: "I'm using the finishing-a-development-branch skill to complete this work."
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- Follow that skill to verify tests, present options, execute choice

## When to Stop and Ask for Help

**STOP executing immediately when:**
- Hit a blocker (missing dependency, test fails, instruction unclear)
- Plan has critical gaps preventing starting
- You don't understand an instruction
- Verification fails repeatedly

**Ask for clarification rather than guessing.**

## When to Revisit Earlier Steps

**Return to Review (Step 1) when:**
- Partner updates the plan based on your feedback
- Fundamental approach needs rethinking

**Don't force through blockers** - stop and ask.

## Remember
- Review plan critically first
- Follow plan steps exactly
- Don't skip verifications
- Reference skills when plan says to
- Stop when blocked, don't guess
- Never start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

```text
1	# VR Docker Baselines — Finish & Merge Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4	
5	**Goal:** Generate Linux/Docker VR baselines, verify the full workflow, and merge branch `fix/vr-pass-with-no-tests` to master.
6	
7	**Architecture:** All VR screenshots are generated inside the official Playwright Docker image (`mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.59.1-noble`) so local and CI environments are identical. Baselines live in `e2e/__snapshots__/` (no platform suffix). The pre-push hook auto-generates baselines on first push if the directory is missing, then compares on subsequent pushes.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** Playwright 1.59.1, Docker, Husky pre-push hook, GitHub Actions
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Current state (read this before starting)
14	
15	- **Worktree:** `/Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/fix-vr-no-tests`
16	- **Branch:** `fix/vr-pass-with-no-tests`
17	- **What's already implemented on this branch:**
18	  - `e2e/visual.spec.ts` — `@visual`-tagged home page screenshot test
19	  - `scripts/vr-docker.mjs` — Docker wrapper (modes: `test`, `update`)
20	  - `package.json` — scripts: `test:vr`, `test:vr:update`, `test:vr:report`
21	  - `playwright.config.ts` — `snapshotPathTemplate: '{testDir}/__snapshots__/{testFilePath}/{arg}{ext}'`
22	  - `.husky/pre-push` — full quality gate with Docker VR check
23	  - `.github/workflows/visual-regression.yml` — CI workflow with `update_snapshots` dispatch
24	  - `CLAUDE.md` — VR workflow docs with agent checklist
25	- **What's missing:** `e2e/__snapshots__/` directory — baselines have NOT been generated yet
26	- **Note:** `feat/pre-push-hook` PR was already merged to master before this branch was cut, so no conflicts expected
27	
28	### Known bugs to fix before baselines can be generated
29	
30	**Bug 1 — Docker VR build fails (`ECONNREFUSED`)**
31	The `playwright.config.ts` `webServer` command runs `yarn build` inside Docker, but the TanStack Start build tries to connect to a local port that isn't available in the container. The fix is to pre-build the app _before_ running Docker (outside the container) and configure the Docker run to serve the pre-built `dist/` instead of rebuilding. Update `scripts/vr-docker.mjs` to:
32	
33	1. Run `APP_BASE_URL=/ yarn build && cp dist/client/_shell.html dist/client/index.html` on the host first
34	2. Then pass `-e SKIP_BUILD=1` (or similar) into Docker so the webServer can skip the build step
35	
36	Or simpler: set `webServer.reuseExistingServer: true` and start the server on the host before launching Docker, then use `--network=host` in the Docker run so the container can reach it.
37	
38	**Bug 2 — `snapshotPathTemplate` missing `{projectName}`**
39	The current template `'{testDir}/__snapshots__/{testFilePath}/{arg}{ext}'` means all three browsers write to the same `home.png`. When `yarn test:e2e` runs (which includes visual tests across all browsers), chromium writes the file first, then firefox/webkit compare against it and get spurious diffs.
40	
41	Fix: add `{projectName}` to the template:
42	
43	```ts
44	snapshotPathTemplate: '{testDir}/__snapshots__/{testFilePath}/{arg}-{projectName}{ext}',
45	```
46	
47	This produces `home-chromium.png`, `home-firefox.png`, `home-webkit.png`. The VR workflow only cares about chromium (`--project=chromium`).
48	
49	**Bug 3 — Visual tests included in `yarn test:e2e`**
50	`yarn test:e2e` runs all tests including `@visual`, but visual tests need baselines that only exist for chromium (Docker). The smoke/a11y tests should be the default E2E suite. Add `--ignore-snapshots` to `test:e2e` or exclude visual tests:
51	
52	```json
53	"test:e2e": "playwright test --ignore-snapshots"
54	```
55	
56	Or tag smoke/a11y differently and filter. The simplest fix: pass `--ignore-snapshots` so visual tests in E2E run don't fail when baselines are stale/missing.
57

66	| `scripts/vr-docker.mjs`                     | Verify — should need no changes              |
67	| `playwright.config.ts`                      | Verify — snapshotPathTemplate must be set    |
68	| `.husky/pre-push`                           | Verify — VR section logic                    |

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env node
2	// scripts/vr-docker.mjs
3	// Runs Playwright VR tests inside the official Playwright Docker image.
4	// Usage:
5	//   node scripts/vr-docker.mjs test    — compare against baselines
6	//   node scripts/vr-docker.mjs update  — regenerate baselines
7	
8	import { execSync, spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';
9	import { readdirSync } from 'node:fs';
10	import path from 'node:path';
11	
12	const PLAYWRIGHT_VERSION = '1.59.1';
13	const DOCKER_IMAGE = `mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v${PLAYWRIGHT_VERSION}-noble`;
14	
15	const mode = process.argv[2];
16	
17	if (mode !== 'test' && mode !== 'update') {
18	  console.error('Usage: node scripts/vr-docker.mjs <test|update>');
19	  process.exit(1);
20	}
21	
22	// ── Check Docker is running ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
23	try {
24	  execSync('docker info', { stdio: 'ignore' });
25	} catch {
26	  console.warn(
27	    '\u001B[33m\u26A0 Docker not running \u2014 cannot execute VR tests.\u001B[0m',
28	  );
29	  process.exit(1);
30	}
31	
32	// ── Pre-build on host (vite build in the container can fail; dist is mounted in) ─
33	const cwd = process.cwd();
34	console.log(
35	  '\u001B[36m\u25B6 Building app on host for Docker VR (APP_BASE_URL=/)\u001B[0m',
36	);
37	execSync(
38	  'APP_BASE_URL=/ yarn build && cp dist/client/_shell.html dist/client/index.html',
39	  { stdio: 'inherit', cwd },
40	);
41	
42	// ── Build the playwright command ──────────────────────────────────────────────
43	const playwrightArgs = [
44	  'npx',
45	  'playwright',
46	  'test',
47	  '--project=chromium',
48	  '--grep',
49	  '@visual',
50	];
51	
52	if (mode === 'update') {
53	  playwrightArgs.push('--update-snapshots');
54	}
55	
56	const shellCommand = [
57	  'yarn install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline',
58	  playwrightArgs.join(' '),
59	].join(' && ');
60	
61	// ── Run inside Docker ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62	
63	const dockerArgs = [
64	  'run',
65	  '--rm',
66	  '--ipc=host',
67	  '-v',
68	  `${cwd}:/work`,
69	  '-w',
70	  '/work',
71	  '-e',
72	  'APP_BASE_URL=/',
73	  '-e',
74	  'VR_DOCKER=1',
75	  DOCKER_IMAGE,
76	  'bash',
77	  '-c',
78	  shellCommand,
79	];
80	
81	console.log(
82	  `\u001B[36m\u25B6 Running VR tests in Docker (${mode} mode)...\u001B[0m`,
83	);
84	
85	const result = spawnSync('docker', dockerArgs, { stdio: 'inherit' });
86	
87	const exitCode = result.status ?? 1;
88	
89	// ── On test failure: surface diff images ──────────────────────────────────────
90	if (mode === 'test' && exitCode !== 0) {
91	  const testResultsDir = path.join(cwd, 'test-results');
92	  const diffFiles = [];
93	
94	  try {
95	    const walk = (dir) => {
96	      for (const entry of readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
97	        const full = path.join(dir, entry.name);
98	        if (entry.isDirectory()) {
99	          walk(full);
100	        } else if (entry.isFile() && entry.name.endsWith('-diff.png')) {
101	          diffFiles.push(full);
102	        }
103	      }
104	    };
105	    walk(testResultsDir);
106	  } catch {
107	    // test-results may not exist
108	  }
109	
110	  if (diffFiles.length > 0) {
111	    console.error('\n\u001B[31m\u2717 Visual diffs detected:\u001B[0m');
112	    for (const f of diffFiles) {
113	      console.error(`  ${f}`);
114	    }
115	  }
116	
117	  console.error(
118	    '\n\u001B[33mTo review the full report: yarn test:vr:report\u001B[0m',
119	  );
120	  console.error(
121	    '\u001B[33mIf the change is intentional, run: yarn test:vr:update\u001B[0m',
122	  );
123	}
124	
125	process.exit(exitCode);
126
```
</details></tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is questioning why we're adding `--ignore-snapshots`. Let me explain my reasoning.

The problem is:
- `e2e.yml` runs ALL tests (including `@visual`) on chromium, firefox, and webkit
- Only chromium baselines exist (that's the Docker VR workflow design)
- When firefox/webkit try to run `toHaveScreenshot('home.png')` in `e2e.yml`, they fail because no baselines exist for `home-firefox.png` or `home-webkit.png`


- Looking at the git status, the chromium baseline is already committed, so the real issue is just that `e2e.yml` is trying to run visual tests across all browsers when only chromium has baselines.

I could either skip snapshot comparisons entirely with `--ignore-snapshots`, exclude visual tests with `--grep-invert @visual`, or run only non-visual tests with `--grep @a11y` or `--grep @smoke`. I'm considering the trade-offs between these approaches—`--ignore-snapshots` would let the tests run as smoke tests without actually validating visuals, whereas excluding visual tests entirely with `--grep-invert @visual` would be cleaner since the VR tests have their own dedicated workflow anyway.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-02 12:59:10Z)**_

Good question. Let me explain the tradeoff:

**Why `--ignore-snapshots`:** The `e2e.yml` runs all tests (including `@visual`) on chromium/firefox/webkit. Only chromium baselines exist (by design — Docker VR is chromium-only). Firefox/webkit fail because `home-firefox.png`/`home-webkit.png` don't exist.

**The alternative:** Use `--grep-invert @visual` to completely exclude visual tests from the E2E workflow. This is actually cleaner — visual regression has its own dedicated `visual-regression.yml`, so `e2e.yml` shouldn't be running them at all.

Which do you prefer?
- **`--ignore-snapshots`** — visual tests still run, snapshot assertions are skipped (test exercises the page without comparing screenshots)
- **`--grep-invert @visual`** — visual tests are excluded from E2E run entirely (cleaner separation of concerns)

---
